interactions.summary: The host asks for a hopeful solution, then asks when the Scofield Bible was written and how Gog and Magog should be classified.
Dugin describes the Scofield Bible as sectarian commentary embedded into scripture that made Russia and Islam appear as end-times enemies for Christians.

Jiang dates Christian Zionism through Herzl, Scofield, and Balfour, then says American fear and anxiety come from Calvinism and that America must return to Christianity and re-examine its foundations; Gog and Magog now appear to be Persia and Russia.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization labels are not reliable in this multi-speaker interview; speaker attribution is inferred from conversational role and named address.

claims.claim: Dugin says the Scofield Bible embedded a small Protestant sect's pseudo-prophecy into scripture, making many American readers unable to distinguish commentary from holy text.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang sequences Christian Zionism through Herzl in 1899, the Scofield Bible in 1909, and Balfour around 1914 as a compressed historical chain.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang says the emotional root of America is fear and anxiety inherited through Calvinism, despite unprecedented material wealth.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang says American redemption would require returning to Christianity and re-examining the principles on which America was built.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang reads Gog and Magog in the present moment as Persia and Russia.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: The Scofield Bible becomes a scripture/commentary confusion machine.
signature_moments.source_phrase: could not distinguish between the authentic biblical text and this embedded so-called pseudo prophecy
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This explains how an annotation becomes a geopolitical imagination.

signature_moments.moment: America is rich like no society in history and still depressed, medicated, and polarized.
signature_moments.source_phrase: one of the most depressed societies in human history
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It keeps Jiang's Calvinism critique tied to lived social psychology.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A 1909 Bible edition/commentary treated here as a vehicle for American dispensationalist Christian Zionism.
An end-times pair that Jiang says is now being interpreted as Persia and Russia.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang gives 1899, 1909, and 1914/Balfour as a tight timeline for Christian Zionism's modern political formation.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang says 1914 for Balfour, but the Balfour Declaration is commonly dated 1917; preserve this as transcript/source wording rather than silently correcting it.
